segments.md:
# The desktop

A working Mac desktop, in Declare — the layers design's
everything-at-once proof (planes.md §11), grown into a demo in its own
right. What's on it:

- **Windows** are a layer: created imperatively (`app.createView` —
  planes.md §7), dragged by their title bars, resized from every edge
  and bottom corner (each with its macOS cursor), raised on activation
  (promotion — order is a slot), shaded/zoomed/closed by real traffic
  lights. One `Window` class carries all of that chrome; three
  subclasses carry the content.
- **Finder** browses this repository's own documentation, its tree
  derived from the docs app's live model — every guide chapter, every
  element in the language reference. Columns scroll, clip, and drag.
- **Viewer** renders any of those documents (real files fetched as
  text; reference pages synthesized from the model). **AppWindow**
  hosts an entire other Declare app as an embedded child — the dock's
  Calendar opens the actual apps/calendar.
- The **dock** magnifies by layout, not paint: each icon's real width
  is a constraint over its resting slot center (the source must never
  read the magnified geometry — that would be a feedback loop), so
  neighbors displace and the plate's edges push outward. Minimized
  windows park in the dock as live thumbnails — dock citizens riding
  the same wave, wearing their app's icon as a badge.
- The **menu bar** is whole menus as records, and contextual: it belongs
  to the front window's application (Files, Markdown, Declare Calendar),
  with the brand mark in the para-Apple slot, About windows, a live
  Window menu, and a Help menu whose search field is a real input inside
  the overlay. ⌘-shortcuts dispatch from the same records.

Icons are drawn (no bitmaps — the weather app's image-elimination
precedent); the open-source icon harvest and the vibrancy material are
the recorded next passes.

// The window classes below are created only at runtime, by name —
// `app.createView("FinderWindow", …)` — never as a declared tag. Class
// reachability is otherwise a static fact (unused classes are dropped, which
// is what tree-shakes production builds), and a name inside a runtime string
// is invisible to that analysis. `use` is the explicit bridge: keep these
// classes registered for creation by name.

use [ FinderWindow, ViewerWindow, AppWindow, AboutWindow, StickyWindow, BrandMark, HelpSearch ]

// ── a desktop window: chrome once, contents per subclass ─────────────────────

class Window extends View [ width = 560, height = 400, clip = true,
    fill = { theme.surface },
    stroke = { stroke(1, theme.line) },

    // shadow inks: the front window floats on a deeper, softer shadow; an
    // inactive one sits closer to the desktop on a lighter, tighter one.
    // (Alpha colors live in declaration position — `#` literals — because the
    // 0x form is defined as 6-digit opaque, so 0x000000XX would paint navy.)

    inkHi: Color = #0000006B,
    inkLo: Color = #00000047,

    shadow = { app.frontWin == this ? shadow(0, 12, 26, this.inkHi != null ? this.inkHi : 0) : shadow(0, 5, 14, this.inkLo != null ? this.inkLo : 0) },

    title: string = "",
    active: boolean = { app.frontWin == this },

    // named geometry — every derived position below reads these, so the
    // numbers exist in exactly one place

    barH: number = 35,                           // title-bar height; content starts below it
    minW: number = 340,                          // resize floors
    minH: number = 120,
    grab: number = 8,                            // edge strip thickness (grabbable band)
    corner: number = 16,                         // corner strip size (wins over the edges)

    // a plain window (an About box) is close-to-dismiss only: no resize, no
    // shade, no zoom — the strips vanish and the yellow/green lights go inert

    plain: boolean = false,

    // custom-chrome windows (Stickies) keep the base's behavior — drag
    // bookkeeping, activation, shade/zoom, resize strips — but drop the
    // standard title bar and grip art, and supply their own chrome on top

    chrome: boolean = true,

    // the macOS-style open zoom: the window grows from the file's position —
    // small at the icon, most of the way within the first frames, settled in
    // under a quarter second; a strong ease-out. bornX/bornY carry the origin in window coords (-1 = no origin:
    // a new window zooms gently from its own center instead).

    bornX: number = -1,
    bornY: number = -1,
    bornFrom: View = null,                       // the view the open came from, for the return trip
    pivotX = { this.bornX >= 0 ? this.bornX : this.width / 2 },
    pivotY = { this.bornY >= 0 ? this.bornY : this.height / 2 },
    born: Animator [ attribute = baseScale, from = { classroot.bornX >= 0 ? 0.15 : 0.85 }, to = 1,
        duration = 230, motion = cubicOut, started = true ],

    // ── the geometry split that makes a minimized window a dock citizen ──
    // Everything imperative (drag, spawn, the zoom animators) writes the base
    // slots wx/wy/baseScale; the real x/y/scale are constraints blending base
    // geometry toward a dock slot the window computes for itself — the same
    // wave math the icons run, over the same live inputs. So a parked window
    // magnifies, displaces, and rides the dock's widening exactly as an icon
    // does: not a placed thumbnail but a member of the wave. `miniT` (the
    // blend, animated 0↔1) is the whole minimize journey.

    wx: number = 0,
    wy: number = 0,
    baseScale: number = 1,                       // the free-window scale — the zoom animators drive this
    dockSlot: number = -1,                       // ≥0 = my slot in the dock's minimized section
    miniT: number = 0,                           // the park blend: 0 = free, 1 = docked (animated — the journey)

    // my slot comes from the one mini layout (app.miniLayout — left-anchored
    // like the icon row, so a magnified thumbnail grows rightward and can
    // never cross the divider): x/w are the slot's live geometry; rw is the
    // resting width, so w/rw is my share of the magnification wave. The
    // macOS treatment: the thumbnail keeps its aspect, sits centered
    // vertically in the cell, and rounds its corners at thumbnail scale.

    slotW: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.dockSlot >= 0 && this.dockSlot < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.dockSlot].w : 48 },
    slotRestW: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.dockSlot >= 0 && this.dockSlot < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.dockSlot].rw : 48 },
    slotX: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.dockSlot >= 0 && this.dockSlot < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.dockSlot].x : 0 },
    parkScale: number = { this.slotW / Math.max(1, this.width) },
    waveMag: number = { this.slotW / Math.max(1, this.slotRestW) },

    // the thumbnail is CENTRED in the plate (measured: real minimized windows
    // centre with ~12px margins, not bottom-anchored like the icons). Plate
    // centre sits 49.5px off the screen bottom (5.5 gap + 44 half-plate); the
    // window's visual centre (parkY + menuBar + halfVisualHeight) lands there.

    // the thumbnail scales around the badge baseline (~14px above the plate
    // floor, screen y = app.height − 19), NOT its own centre — so on rollover
    // it grows UPWARD (its bottom moves away from the plate floor, never toward
    // it), matching the real dock. At rest (waveMag 1) this is the plate-centre
    // (…− 49.5…), so a resting thumbnail is still centred.

    parkY: number = { app.height - 19 - 30.5 * this.waveMag - this.height * this.parkScale / 2 - app.menuBarH },

    baseRadius: number = 10,                     // the window's normal corner rounding
    thumbRadius: number = 3.5,                   // …and its rounding at thumbnail scale, in dock pixels
    cornerRadius = { this.baseRadius + (this.thumbRadius * this.waveMag / Math.max(0.02, this.parkScale) - this.baseRadius) * this.miniT },

    x = { this.wx + (this.slotX - this.wx) * this.miniT },
    y = { this.wy + (this.parkY - this.wy) * this.miniT },
    scale = { this.baseScale + (this.parkScale - this.baseScale) * this.miniT },

    // each journey stops the other before starting (a restore mid-park must
    // not leave two animators racing miniT), and each completion handler
    // verifies it actually arrived — an interrupted animator's stop must
    // never commit the destination's state (the phantom-slot bug)

    miniGo: Animator [ attribute = miniT, to = 1, duration = 260, motion = cubicIn,
        onStop() { app.nudge() },                // no state — just a re-derive poke for seat checks
        ],
    miniBack: Animator [ attribute = miniT, to = 0, duration = 260, motion = cubicOut,
        onStop() { if (classroot.miniT <= 0.01) app.unparked(classroot) },
        ],

    // …and the open runs in reverse on close: the window shrinks back to its
    // return spot — recomputed at close time from the origin view (the finder
    // may have moved), falling back to the birth snapshot if it's gone.

    closing: boolean = false,

    beginClose() {
        if (closing) return
        closing = true
        if (bornFrom != null) {
            let ox = bornFrom.width / 2
            let oy = bornFrom.height / 2
            let n = bornFrom
            let top = null
            while (n != null) { ox += n.x || 0; oy += n.y || 0; top = n; n = n.parent }
            // the walk reaching app proves the origin view is still attached;
            // its result is app coords — shift into this window's frame
            if (top == app) { bornX = ox - this.x; bornY = oy - this.y - app.menuBarH }
            }
        this.die.start()
        },

    die: Animator [ attribute = baseScale, to = 0.15, duration = 200, motion = cubicIn,
        onStop() { app.reallyClose(classroot) },
        ],

    // drag/resize bookkeeping (the captured-move pattern: root-space anchors)

    px0: number = 0,
    py0: number = 0,
    wx0: number = 0,
    wy0: number = 0,
    wh0: number = 0,
    dragging: boolean = false,
    sizeMode: string = "",                       // "" | "r" | "b" | "rb"

    // shade (Stickies' collapse widget) and zoom (green) remember what they
    // displace; minimize (yellow) is the Mac's — the window zooms down into
    // the dock's minimized-windows section (the open/close animation's curve,
    // aimed at the tile) and back out when its tile is clicked

    shaded: boolean = false,
    zoomed: boolean = false,
    minimized: boolean = false,
    saveH: number = 0,
    saveX: number = 0,
    saveY: number = 0,
    saveW: number = 0,

    // minimize does not hide: the window parks at its dock tile, scaled to
    // fit — a live thumbnail (an embedded app inside keeps running; its DOM
    // island never unmounts, only transforms). The tile above it is the
    // click target, so the parked window can't be poked at 8% scale.

    // activating any real window dismisses open About boxes — they're modals
    // in spirit: clicking anywhere else lets them go (the red light works too)

    activate() { app.frontWin = this; this.raise(app.lowestMini()); if (!plain) app.dismissAbouts() },
    onMouseDown() { this.activate() },

    // subclass content attaches after the base's children, so any content that
    // reaches the window's edges would eat the resize strips' events — the
    // chrome re-promotes above everything (order is a slot). Subclasses that
    // override onInit call raiseChrome() themselves.

    raiseChrome() {
        this.edgeL.raise()
        this.edgeR.raise()
        this.edgeB.raise()
        this.cornerBL.raise()
        this.grip.raise()
        this.veil.raise()                        // topmost of all — a background window's first-click catcher
        },

    onInit() { this.raiseChrome() },

    // shade collapses the window to its bar (the old macOS window-shade);
    // zoom fills the desktop, leaving the dock's band clear at the bottom

    shadeToggle() {
        if (shaded) { this.height = saveH; shaded = false }
        else { saveH = this.height; this.height = barH; shaded = true }
        },

    zoomToggle() {
        if (zoomed) { this.wx = saveX; this.wy = saveY; this.width = saveW; this.height = saveH; zoomed = false }
        else {
            saveX = this.wx; saveY = this.wy; saveW = this.width; saveH = this.height
            this.wx = 12; this.wy = 8
            this.width = app.width - 24
            this.height = app.height - 130            // 130 ≈ the dock band + margins
            zoomed = true
            }
        },

    wl0: number = 0,                             // window x at size start (left-edge modes)

    beginSize(mode) {
        if (plain) return
        this.activate()
        sizeMode = "" + mode
        px0 = app.pointerX
        py0 = app.pointerY
        wx0 = this.width
        wh0 = this.height
        wl0 = this.wx
        },

    // modes compose macOS's grabbable set: "l" | "r" | "b" | "lb" | "rb".
    // A left-edge drag keeps the right edge fixed: x follows the width.

    moveSize() {
        if (sizeMode == "") return
        const dx = app.pointerX - px0
        const dy = app.pointerY - py0
        if (sizeMode.indexOf("r") >= 0) this.width = Math.max(minW, wx0 + dx)
        if (sizeMode.indexOf("l") >= 0) {
            const w = Math.max(minW, wx0 - dx)
            this.wx = wl0 + (wx0 - w)
            this.width = w
            }
        if (sizeMode.indexOf("b") >= 0) this.height = Math.max(minH, wh0 + dy)
        },

    lightsHover: boolean = false,

    bar: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = { classroot.barH }, visible = { classroot.chrome },

        // the active bar carries a subtle vertical gradient (the Finder read);
        // inactive drops to the flat background tone

        fill = { classroot.active
                ? (app.darkMode ? gradient(0x3B3B40, 0x2E2E32) : gradient(0xFAFAFC, 0xE6E6EA))
                : theme.bg },
        onMouseDown() {
            classroot.activate()
            classroot.dragging = true
            classroot.px0 = app.pointerX
            classroot.py0 = app.pointerY
            classroot.wx0 = classroot.wx
            classroot.wy0 = classroot.wy
            },
        onMouseMove() {
            if (!classroot.dragging) return
            // clamped so a window can never be dragged beyond reach: some of
            // it stays on the desktop on every side
            classroot.wx = Math.max(-200, Math.min(app.width - 60, classroot.wx0 + app.pointerX - classroot.px0))
            classroot.wy = Math.max(0, Math.min(app.height - 90, classroot.wy0 + app.pointerY - classroot.py0))
            },
        onMouseUp() { classroot.dragging = false },

        // the traffic lights — all three real: close, shade, zoom. Grey when
        // the window is inactive. The rollover state belongs to the lights
        // block alone (the bounding rectangle of the three circles — David's
        // reference), not the bar: hovering it colorizes even an inactive
        // window's lights and surfaces the glyphs; the rest of the bar never
        // does.

        // 14px circles on a 20px pitch (the macOS proportions), so the
        // block is 2·20 + 14 = 54 wide, centered in the 35px bar

        lights: View [ x = 13, y = 10.5, width = 54, height = 14,
            onMouseOver() { classroot.lightsHover = true },
            onMouseOut()  { classroot.lightsHover = false },
            close: TrafficLight [ x = 0, c = #EC6A5E, rim = #D25349, ink = #7B1006, sym = "close",
                on = { classroot.active || classroot.lightsHover }, showGlyph = { classroot.lightsHover },
                onMouseOver() { classroot.lightsHover = true },
                onMouseOut()  { classroot.lightsHover = false },
                onClick() { app.closeWin(classroot) },
                ],
            mini: TrafficLight [ x = 20, c = #F5BF4F, rim = #D9A340, ink = #8F5B00, sym = "min",
                on = { classroot.active || classroot.lightsHover }, showGlyph = { classroot.lightsHover },
                onMouseOver() { classroot.lightsHover = true },
                onMouseOut()  { classroot.lightsHover = false },
                onClick() { if (!classroot.plain) app.minimizeWin(classroot) },
                ],
            zoom: TrafficLight [ x = 40, c = #61C554, rim = #4CAF45, ink = #145E14, sym = "zoom",
                on = { classroot.active || classroot.lightsHover }, showGlyph = { classroot.lightsHover },
                onMouseOver() { classroot.lightsHover = true },
                onMouseOut()  { classroot.lightsHover = false },
                onClick() { if (!classroot.plain) classroot.zoomToggle() },
                ],
            ],

        cap: Text [ x = center, y = center, fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold,
            textColor = { classroot.active ? theme.text : theme.textMuted },
            text = { classroot.title } ],

        // the separator under the title bar — present active or not

        sep: View [ x = 0, y = { classroot.barH - 1 }, width = { parent.width }, height = 1, fill = { theme.line } ],
        ],

    // resize strips — every edge and corner macOS makes grabbable except the
    // top (the title bar owns it): left/right edges, bottom edge, and the two
    // bottom corners, each with its macOS cursor. Three unique shapes: ↔ for
    // either vertical edge, ↕ for the bottom, and the two diagonals for the
    // corners. The corners overlap the edges' ends and win the hit-test.

    edgeL: View [ x = 0, y = { classroot.barH }, width = { classroot.grab }, visible = { !classroot.plain && !classroot.shaded },
        height = { parent.height - classroot.barH - classroot.corner }, cursor = "ew-resize",
        onMouseDown() { classroot.beginSize("l") },
        onMouseMove() { classroot.moveSize() },
        onMouseUp()   { classroot.sizeMode = "" },
        ],
    edgeR: View [ x = { parent.width - classroot.grab }, y = { classroot.barH }, width = { classroot.grab }, visible = { !classroot.plain && !classroot.shaded },
        height = { parent.height - classroot.barH - classroot.corner }, cursor = "ew-resize",
        onMouseDown() { classroot.beginSize("r") },
        onMouseMove() { classroot.moveSize() },
        onMouseUp()   { classroot.sizeMode = "" },
        ],
    edgeB: View [ x = { classroot.corner }, y = { parent.height - classroot.grab }, visible = { !classroot.plain && !classroot.shaded },
        width = { parent.width - 2 * classroot.corner }, height = { classroot.grab }, cursor = "ns-resize",
        onMouseDown() { classroot.beginSize("b") },
        onMouseMove() { classroot.moveSize() },
        onMouseUp()   { classroot.sizeMode = "" },
        ],
    cornerBL: View [ x = 0, y = { parent.height - classroot.corner }, visible = { !classroot.plain && !classroot.shaded },
        width = { classroot.corner }, height = { classroot.corner }, cursor = "nesw-resize",
        onMouseDown() { classroot.beginSize("lb") },
        onMouseMove() { classroot.moveSize() },
        onMouseUp()   { classroot.sizeMode = "" },
        ],

    // the bottom-right corner also draws its affordance (the classic grip)

    grip: View [ x = { parent.width - classroot.corner }, y = { parent.height - classroot.corner }, visible = { !classroot.plain && !classroot.shaded },
        width = { classroot.corner }, height = { classroot.corner }, cursor = "nwse-resize",
        onMouseDown() { classroot.beginSize("rb") },
        onMouseMove() { classroot.moveSize() },
        onMouseUp()   { classroot.sizeMode = "" },
        draw(d) {
            if (!classroot.chrome) return            // custom chrome resizes here too, but unmarked
            d.strokeStyle = theme.textFaint
            d.lineWidth = 1
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo(6, 14); d.lineTo(14, 6)
            d.moveTo(10, 14); d.lineTo(14, 10)
            d.stroke()
            },
        ],

    // ── the first-click veil (the macOS rule, and it is swappable —
    // app.firstClickActivatesOnly): a background window's content is covered
    // by this transparent view, so a click there raises the window and goes
    // no further — the content (built-in or an embedded app's island alike)
    // never hears it, because the veil is the hit target and events do not
    // bubble. The chrome stays live: the title bar, lights, and this veil
    // itself all activate. It sits topmost (raiseChrome) and only exists
    // while the window is in the background, so an active window's content
    // receives everything as before.

    veil: View [ visible = { !classroot.active && app.firstClickActivatesOnly },
        x = 0, y = { classroot.barH },
        width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height - classroot.barH },
        onMouseDown() { classroot.activate() },
        ],
    ]


// The traffic lights, macOS-style: hairline rim in a darker
// tone of the fill (never black), and the hover glyphs are Apple's drawn
// iconography in a dark tone of the same hue — thick rounded ×, − bar, and
// the fullscreen pair of triangles split along the "/" diagonal.

class TrafficLight extends View [ y = 0, width = 14, height = 14, cornerRadius = 7,
    c:   Color = #EC6A5E,
    rim: Color = #D25349,
    ink: Color = #7B1006,
    sym: string = "",                            // "close" | "min" | "zoom"
    on: boolean = false,                         // the window's active state, fed at the use site
    showGlyph: boolean = false,                  // bar-hover, fed at the use site

    // the rims are hairlines (David's reference: "very thin and subtle") — a
    // half-pixel stroke, hue-toned when lit, barely-there grey when not

    fill = { on ? c : (app.darkMode ? 0x54545A : 0xD2D2D6) },
    faint: Color = #00000014,
    stroke = { stroke(0.5, on && rim != null ? rim : (this.faint != null ? this.faint : 0)) },

    // glyph paths are authored in the light's own 14-box

    g: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 14, height = 14,
        visible = { classroot.showGlyph && classroot.on },
        draw(d) {
            d.strokeStyle = classroot.ink
            d.fillStyle = classroot.ink
            d.lineCap = "round"
            if (classroot.sym == "close") {
                d.lineWidth = 1.6
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(4.5, 4.5); d.lineTo(9.5, 9.5)
                d.moveTo(9.5, 4.5); d.lineTo(4.5, 9.5)
                d.stroke()
                }
            else if (classroot.sym == "min") {
                d.lineWidth = 1.8
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(3.5, 7); d.lineTo(10.5, 7)
                d.stroke()
                }
            else if (classroot.sym == "zoom") {
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(4.1, 4.1); d.lineTo(8.5, 4.1); d.lineTo(4.1, 8.5)
                d.closePath(); d.fill()
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(9.9, 9.9); d.lineTo(5.5, 9.9); d.lineTo(9.9, 5.5)
                d.closePath(); d.fill()
                }
            },
        ],
    ]


// ── the brand mark: Declare's identity in the para-Apple slot ────────────────
//
// The mark — a rounded square holding the two brackets — rendered
// monochrome for the menu bar, the way the Apple logo is: solid menu-bar ink,
// brackets knocked out. (The gradient version stays the social-card identity;
// a colored mark would shout in a monochrome bar.)

class BrandMark extends View [ width = 18, height = 18, cornerRadius = 9,
    fill = { theme.text },

    // two spaces between the brackets (the dock icon's mark), shrunk to fit the circle

    Text [ x = center, y = center, fontSize = 8, fontWeight = bold,
        textColor = { theme.surface }, text = "[  ]" ],
    ]


// ── the Help menu's search field — a real input living in a menu overlay ─────
//
// Rendered as a Menu `kind:` row: the row's chrome (no highlight, no pick) is
// the menu's; inside is the library SearchField — the macOS pill — with a
// live TextInput: keyboard focus inside a raised layer, the case David
// flagged as the interesting focus test.

class HelpSearch extends View [ height = 40,
    width = { parent.width },

    // the field reaches to 12px from the menu's edges,
    // past the kind-row host's standard 24px inset — hence the negative x

    sf: SearchField [ x = -12, y = 5, width = { parent.width + 10 }, height = 30 ],
    ]


// ── About windows: the Apple treatment — vertical, an image, close to dismiss ─
//
// One class serves the desktop and all three applications: `forApp` picks the
// art, the name, and the blurb. `plain` windows (About boxes) give up resize
// and shade/zoom — the close light is the whole interface, as on the Mac.

class AboutWindow extends Window [ width = 280, height = 380, plain = true,
    forApp: string = "desktop",                  // desktop | files | markdown | calendar | declareviewer | stickies

    aname: string = { this.forApp == "files" ? "Files"
        : this.forApp == "markdown" ? "Markdown"
        : this.forApp == "calendar" ? "Declare Calendar"
        : this.forApp == "declareviewer" ? "Declare Viewer"
        : this.forApp == "stickies" ? "Stickies" : "Declare Desktop" },

    blurb: string = { this.forApp == "files" ? "Browses the live documentation model — every chapter, every element."
        : this.forApp == "markdown" ? "Renders the real files, and reference pages synthesized from the model."
        : this.forApp == "declareviewer" ? "Reader, source, and live editor over any Declare program — here, hosted as an embedded app."
        : this.forApp == "calendar" ? "The actual apps/calendar, running as an embedded child app in this window's island."
        : this.forApp == "stickies" ? "Notes with their own window chrome, in the classic Stickies spirit — built into this program."
        : "A Mac desktop, declared. Windows, dock, and menus are views and records, all drawn — no bitmaps, no borrowed art." },

    body: View [ x = 0, y = { classroot.barH }, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height - classroot.barH },
        col: View [ x = 24, y = 22, width = { parent.width - 48 },
            layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 10 ],

            art: View [ x = center, width = 96, height = 96, cornerRadius = 22, clip = true,
                fill = { classroot.forApp == "markdown" ? gradient(0x8E7CE8, 0x5A46B8)
                    : classroot.forApp == "files" ? gradient(0x59B0F6, 0x1D6FD1)
                    : classroot.forApp == "calendar" ? gradient(0xFFFFFF, 0xEFEFF2)
                    : classroot.forApp == "declareviewer" ? gradient(0x2E6FE0, 0x12A594)
                    : classroot.forApp == "stickies" ? gradient(0xFCF4A7, 0xF3E27E)
                    : gradient(0x4AA8E8, 0x35C4B5) },

                // per-app art, authored in a 48-box and doubled (the dock's rule)

// ── small drawn Finder iconography: the macOS folder, and the dog-eared page ─

class FolderIcon extends View [ width = 17, height = 13,
    draw(d) {
        const rr = (x, y, w, h, r) => {
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo(x + r, y)
            d.lineTo(x + w - r, y); d.arc(x + w - r, y + r, r, -Math.PI / 2, 0, false)
            d.lineTo(x + w, y + h - r); d.arc(x + w - r, y + h - r, r, 0, Math.PI / 2, false)
            d.lineTo(x + r, y + h); d.arc(x + r, y + h - r, r, Math.PI / 2, Math.PI, false)
            d.lineTo(x, y + r); d.arc(x + r, y + r, r, Math.PI, Math.PI * 1.5, false)
            d.closePath()

        d.fillStyle = 0x4E9FEA
        rr(0, 0, 7.5, 4, 1.5); d.fill()              // the back tab
        d.fillStyle = 0x6AB2F5
        rr(0, 1.8, 17, 11, 1.5); d.fill()            // the body

// ── AppWindow: a full Declare app in a desktop window ────────────────────────
//
// The island carries `slot = "run:<path>"` — the page host's preview machinery
// (browser/host-client.js, the same pipeline behind the homepage and docs
// islands) fetches the program, compiles it on the boot ladder (prewarm →
// cache → in-browser compile; identical on the dev server and the static
// host), and renders it as an embedded child app: its own reactive graph, its
// own input router (the app-root ownership rule), sized to the island's box.
// The slot name is demos/-relative, so "../../calendar/calendar" reaches the
// real app — no copy, no shim.

class AppWindow extends Window [ width = 880, height = 560,
    appPath: string = "",
    extraEnv: string = "",                       // appended env parameters ("program=…")

    // the slot's ENV segment (after `|`) is the embedding environment — the
    // host parses it into the child's reactive `app.env`, at mount and on
    // every change: toggling the desktop's appearance re-marks the slot and
    // the hosted app re-derives (its Auto mode follows `app.env.dark`)

    // a press anywhere in the hosted app also lands here (the router remaps
    // embedded presses to the island — dom-backend), so clicking the child's
    // content raises this window exactly as clicking any window body does

    island: DOMIsland [ x = 0, y = { classroot.barH }, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height - classroot.barH },
        onMouseDown() { classroot.activate() },
        slot = { "run:" + classroot.appPath + "|dark=" + (app.darkMode ? "1" : "0")
            + (classroot.extraEnv != "" ? "&" + classroot.extraEnv : "") },
        ],
    ]

    inline: string = { this.path.indexOf("ref:") == 0 ? app.buildRef(this.path.slice(4)) : "" },

    src: DataSource [ format = "text", url = { classroot.path } ],

    // the scrolling body is a real pointer target covering the whole window,
    // and subclass children attach after the base's — so the Window's resize
    // strips re-promote above it (order is a slot) or edge drags never land

    onInit() {

// ── the Stickies note: a custom window type in the classic Stickies
// spirit. Square corners; #FEF49C paper; the active
// state grows a saturated #FEEA3D title strip carrying olive-outlined pale
// widgets — the close box at the left, the zoom triangle and the collapse
// box at the right — and the inactive note is chromeless: strip and widgets
// vanish into the paper. All the base Window's behavior (drag bookkeeping,
// activation shadow, shade, resize edges) carries through `chrome = false` —
// the styling system's proof that chrome is a skin, not the skeleton. ──────

class StickyWindow extends Window [ width = 300, height = 250, chrome = false,
    baseRadius = 0, barH = 12, minW = 160, minH = 90,
    title = "",                                  // untitled — labels fall back to the app's name
    note: string = "",                           // the note's seed text

    // the note's palette: paper #FEF49C, active strip #FEEA3D, widget
    // linework #DBC501 dark olive over #FFF49C pale, and a half-pixel
    // #4E4E52 rim — a full CSS pixel reads as drawn-on lines, not an edge

    fill = #FEF49C,
    stroke = { stroke(0.5, 0x4E4E52) },
    inkHi = #00000052,
    inkLo = #00000038,
    shadow = { app.frontWin == this ? shadow(0, 10, 26, this.inkHi != null ? this.inkHi : 0) : shadow(0, 3, 9, this.inkLo != null ? this.inkLo : 0) },

    strip: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { parent.width }, height = { classroot.barH },
        fill = { classroot.active ? 0xFEEA3D : 0xFEF49C },
        onMouseDown() {
            classroot.activate()
            classroot.dragging = true
            classroot.px0 = app.pointerX
            classroot.py0 = app.pointerY
            classroot.wx0 = classroot.wx
            classroot.wy0 = classroot.wy
            },
        onMouseMove() {
            if (!classroot.dragging) return
            classroot.wx = Math.max(-200, Math.min(app.width - 60, classroot.wx0 + app.pointerX - classroot.px0))
            classroot.wy = Math.max(0, Math.min(app.height - 90, classroot.wy0 + app.pointerY - classroot.py0))
            },
        onMouseUp() { classroot.dragging = false },

        // the strip's widgets: dark olive outlines around pale interiors —
        // the close box 11px in from the left, the zoom triangle (right
        // angle at bottom-right) and the collapse box in from the right

        closeBox: View [ visible = { classroot.active }, x = 11, y = 2.25, width = 7.5, height = 7.5,
            fill = #FFF49C, stroke = { stroke(1.4, 0xDBC501) },
            onClick() { app.closeWin(classroot) },
            ],
        zoomW: View [ visible = { classroot.active }, x = { parent.width - 33 }, y = 2.25, width = 8, height = 8,
            onClick() { classroot.zoomToggle() },
            draw(d) {
                d.fillStyle = 0xFFF49C
                d.strokeStyle = 0xDBC501
                d.lineWidth = 1.4
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(0.7, 7.3); d.lineTo(7.3, 7.3); d.lineTo(7.3, 0.7)
                d.closePath(); d.fill(); d.stroke()
                },
            ],
        collapseW: View [ visible = { classroot.active }, x = { parent.width - 21 }, y = 2.25, width = 8, height = 8,
            onClick() { classroot.shadeToggle() },   // collapse-to-strip is the Stickies move
            draw(d) {
                d.fillStyle = 0xFFF49C
                d.fillRect(0.7, 0.7, 6.6, 6.6)
                d.strokeStyle = 0xDBC501
                d.lineWidth = 1.4
                d.beginPath()
                d.rect(0.7, 0.7, 6.6, 6.6)
                d.stroke()
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(0.7, 3.1); d.lineTo(7.3, 3.1)
                d.stroke()
                },
            ],
        ],

    // the note body is formatted — a Markdown field on the paper (no input
    // chrome, no focus ring; the note's text is authored, not typed)

    pad: View [ x = 12, y = { classroot.barH + 6 }, clip = true,
        width = { parent.width - 24 }, height = { parent.height - classroot.barH - 16 },
        md: Markdown [ width = { parent.width }, dark = false,
            bodyColor = 0x1D1D1F, scale = 0.82, lineHeight = 1.45,
            text = { classroot.note } ],
        ],
    ]


// ── a minimized window's dock tile: an invisible click target that runs the
// same magnification wave as the icons (so its hit area and label track the
// growing thumbnail beneath) — the visual is the window itself, parked live
// at this slot. The tile sits in the dock layer above the parked window, so
// a click restores instead of poking the tiny content; its pill is the
// DockIcon's, showing the window's title (or its app's name, untitled). ─────

class MiniTile extends View [

    // a static pool member (no replication, no Dataset): `ix` is assigned at
    // the use site, and every property below is a pure constraint over the
    // app's mini state — one atomic write to miniRecs re-derives the whole
    // tile, so no per-row machinery exists to go stale

    ix: number = 0,
    on: boolean = { app.miniRecs != null && this.ix < app.miniRecs.length },
    visible = { this.on },
    lbl: string = { this.on ? "" + app.miniRecs[this.ix].label : "" },
    kind: string = { this.on ? "" + app.miniRecs[this.ix].kind : "" },
    hovered: boolean = false,

    // geometry straight from the mini layout (the same entries the parked
    // window reads), translated into the minis section's local space

    slotW: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.ix < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.ix].w : 48 },
    slotH: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.ix < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.ix].h : 48 },
    slotRestW: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.ix < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.ix].rw : 48 },
    waveMag: number = { this.slotW / Math.max(1, this.slotRestW) },
    x = { (app.miniLayout != null && this.ix < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.ix].x : 0) - (app.dock.x + app.dock.row.width + 32) },
    width = { this.slotW },
    height = { this.slotH },   // the thumbnail's aspect height (not icon-square), so the badge lands on the thumbnail's own corner
    y = { parent.height - 13.5 - 30.5 * this.waveMag - this.height / 2 },   // scales around the badge baseline (grows upward on rollover), = plate-centre at rest — matches the parked window

// page scroll — a desktop never scrolls), and the root clips, so windows
// hanging past an edge are cropped where they lie, never moved. Everything

    theme = { Themes.cupertino(app.darkMode) },
    fontFamily = "-apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif",

    // the wallpaper — a plain gradient, darkened with the appearance

    fill = { app.darkMode ? gradient(0x2A3A55, 0x18222F) : gradient(0x7FA8D9, 0x4A6FA5) },

    //     sparse pointer events. Its slight trail is the settle you feel.

    //     whole wave, so entering and leaving glide instead of snapping.

        // capture pipeline both sides): the peak is HIGHER (~1.55×) and the
        // first-step drop STEEPER than the old broad curve — a pointed mountain,
        // not a dome. peak · ±1 · ±2 · ±3 · ±4 = 1.55 · 1.35 · 1.18 · 1.06 · 1.0.
        const ys = [1.55, 1.35, 1.18, 1.06, 1]
        if (a >= xs[4]) return 1
        for (let i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
            if (a <= xs[i + 1]) return ys[i] + (ys[i + 1] - ys[i]) * (a - xs[i]) / (xs[i + 1] - xs[i])

            else if (activeApp == "calendar") this.openApp("../../calendar/calendar", "Calendar", null, "base=../calendar/")
            else if (activeApp == "declareviewer") this.openApp("../../viewer/viewer", "Declare Viewer", null, "program=desktop.declare")

        else if (id == "open") {
            if (activeApp == "declareviewer") this.openApp("../../viewer/viewer", "Declare Viewer", null, "program=desktop.declare")
            else if (activeApp == "stickies") this.newSticky("", null)
            else this.openViewer("../../docs/declare.md", "declare.md")
            }
        else if (id == "close") { if (frontWin != null) this.closeWin(frontWin) }
        else if (id == "dark") app.darkMode = !app.darkMode
        else if (id == "about-desktop") this.openAbout("desktop")
        else if (id == "about-app") this.openAbout(this.activeApp)
        else if (id == "source-desktop") this.openSource("desktop")
        else if (id == "source") this.openSource(this.activeApp)
        else if (id == "quit") this.quitApp(this.activeApp)
        else if (id.indexOf("win:") == 0) this.focusWin(this.wins.children[+id.slice(4)])
        },

    focusWin(w) { if (w == null) return; if (this.isMin(w)) this.restoreMini(w); else w.activate() },

    // Quit closes every window of that application — each by its own reverse
    // zoom — and clears its running flag (Files has no Quit: like the Finder,
    // it's always running). Running is an application state, not a window
    // count: closing the last window leaves the app running (and its dock
    // dot lit), exactly as macOS does — only Quit ends it.

    quitApp(a) {
        const doomed = this.wins.children.filter((w) => this.appOf(w) == a)
        for (const w of doomed) this.closeWin(w)
        if (a == "markdown") mdRunning = false
        else if (a == "calendar") calRunning = false
        else if (a == "declareviewer") dvRunning = false
        else if (a == "stickies") stRunning = false
        },

    // a dock icon's click, macOS's way: a running app's icon raises its open
    // windows (in their stacking order, so the front one ends front); with
    // none open — running or not — the app's default open runs instead

    dockOpen(a, open) {
        const live = this.wins.children.filter((w) => this.appOf(w) == a && !this.isPlain(w) && !this.isMin(w))
        if (live.length > 0) { for (const w of live) this.focusWin(w); return }
        // nothing up but something in the dock: restore the most recent tile
        const mins = this.miniList().filter((w) => this.appOf(w) == a)
        if (mins.length > 0) { this.restoreMini(mins[mins.length - 1]); return }
        open()
        },

    // ── minimize-to-dock (the Mac's): a minimized window becomes a dock
    // citizen — its geometry constraints (Window.miniCx …) run the icons'
    // own wave math, so it magnifies and displaces with the row; the dock's
    // span is sprung, so the plate widens smoothly to admit it. The records
    // here drive the invisible hit tiles and their label pills. ────────────

    isMin(w) { return w != null && w.dockSlot != null && w.dockSlot >= 0 },
    ixOf(w) { return w != null && w.dockSlot != null ? +w.dockSlot : -1 },
    setIx(w, i) { w.dockSlot = i },
    parkOf(w) { return w != null && w.miniT != null ? +w.miniT : 0 },
    labelFor(w) { const t = this.titleOf(w); return t != "" ? t : this.nameOf(this.appOf(w)) },
    miniList() { return this.wins.children.filter((w) => this.isMin(w)).sort((a, b) => this.ixOf(a) - this.ixOf(b)) },

    miniCount: number = 0,
    miniRecs: array = null,

    // compact the slot indices and rebuild the tile records — after every
    // park, unpark, and close-of-parked. Slot widths are per window: the
    // thumbnail keeps its window's aspect, its longer edge matching an app
    // icon's edge, so it is never wider or taller than an icon.

    sizeOf(w) { return ({ w: w.width, h: w.height }) },

    // miniWins mirrors the records by index with the window references — a
    // parallel app attr, kept out of the records: records are data, and a
    // View's circular graph must never enter data-shaped state

    miniWins: array = null,

    rebuildMinis() {
        const ws = this.miniList()
        const out = []
        let total = 0
        for (let i = 0; i < ws.length; i++) {
            this.setIx(ws[i], i)
            const z = this.sizeOf(ws[i])
            // the longer edge matches an app icon's edge: a minimized
            // window is never wider or taller than an icon
            const fit = this.thumbEdge / Math.max(z.w, z.h)
            const slotW = Math.round(z.w * fit)
            const slotH = Math.round(z.h * fit)
            out.push(({ ix: i, label: this.labelFor(ws[i]), kind: this.appOf(ws[i]), w: slotW, h: slotH }))
            total += slotW + 8
            }
        miniRecs = out
        miniWins = ws
        miniCount = out.length
        // the mini section's resting width added to the plate: the divider zone
        // (34 = 16px left margin + line + 18px right) plus the slots and their
        // gaps. `total` already counts a trailing 8px gap, so: total + 34 − 8.
        miniGoal = out.length > 0 ? total + 26 : 0
        },

    // the minimized section's width — sprung toward the rebuilt goal, so
    // admitting or releasing a tile widens and narrows the dock as motion

    miniGoal: number = 0,
    miniSpan: number = 0,
    spanSpring: Spring [ attribute = miniSpan, to = { app.miniGoal }, stiffness = 380, damping = 34, epsilon = 0.5 ],

    // ── the mini layout: one computation both the parked windows and their
    // hit tiles read (deps arrive as arguments, so the constraint visibly
    // tracks them — the windowItems pattern). Left-anchored accumulation,
    // exactly like the icon row's layout: a magnified thumbnail pushes its
    // neighbors right and never crosses the divider on its left. The wave's
    // falloff runs over resting centers (the icons' anti-feedback rule).

    miniLayout: array = { this.computeMiniLayout(app.miniRecs, app.dockX, app.dock.hot,
        app.dock.x, app.dock.row.width, app.miniSpan, app.width) },

    // iconRow = the nine icons + eight gaps at rest; 10 = the plate's side
    // skirts (5 each); 5 = the row's inset in the plate; 17 = the divider
    // zone (8 + line + 8). The wave itself is app.dockMag — one curve, read
    // here for the tiles and by each DockIcon for the icons.

    computeMiniLayout(recs, px, hot, dockLeftX, rowW, span, appW) {
        const rs = recs || []
        const iconRow = 9 * app.iconEdge + 8 * 16   // the nine icons + eight gaps, at rest
        // 32 = the plate's two 16px side skirts; 16 = the row's inset in the
        // plate; 34 = the divider zone (16px left margin + line + 18px right
        // margin) — the first thumbnail starts 34px past the last icon's edge
        const restDockX = (appW - (iconRow + 32 + span)) / 2
        let rx = restDockX + 16 + iconRow + 34      // first slot's resting left
        let lx = dockLeftX + 16 + rowW + 34         // …and its live left
        const out = []
        for (const r of rs) {
            const cx0 = rx + r.w / 2
            // the same wave the icons ride (app.dockMag), so tiles magnify in step
            const m = 1 + (app.dockMag(px - cx0) - 1) * hot
            const w = r.w * m
            out.push(({ x: lx, w: w, rw: r.w, h: (r.h || r.w) * m, cx: lx + w / 2 }))
            lx += w + 8
            rx += r.w + 8
            }
        return out
        },

    // the wave's extra width beyond rest — the plate stretches for it, so a
    // magnified thumbnail never hangs past the dock's right edge

    miniMagX: number = { this.magOf(app.miniLayout, app.miniRecs) },
    magOf(lay, recs) {
        if (lay == null || recs == null || lay.length == 0) return 0
        let d = 0
        for (let i = 0; i < lay.length; i++) d += lay[i].w - recs[i].w
        return Math.max(0, d)
        },

    startPark(w) { w.miniBack.stop(); w.bornX = 0; w.bornY = 0; w.miniGo.start() },
    startUnpark(w) { w.miniGo.stop(); w.miniBack.start() },

    // settled-park state rides the records (the badge derives from its
    // record's `full`, plain data — no reactive aggregation in constraints):
    // full only after the arriving journey completes, and cleared the
    // instant the journey home begins

    homingOf(w) { return w != null && w.homing == true },
    setHoming(w, v) { w.homing = v },

    minimizeWin(w) {
        if (w == null || this.isPlain(w) || this.isMin(w)) return
        if (this.miniCount >= 6) return          // the static tile pool's size
        this.setIx(w, this.miniCount)
        this.rebuildMinis()
        if (frontWin == w) frontWin = null
        w.raise()                                // the thumbnail pins to the top of the minimized band
        this.startPark(w)
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        },

    bringFront(w) { w.activate() },              // any-seam (focusWin would recurse via isMin)

    // ── the minimized band (the z-order fix): minimized windows' live
    // thumbnails stay in `wins` (below the plate) but pin to the TOP of the
    // layer, so a full-size window that reaches the dock can't cover them. A
    // window that activates or opens raises only to just BENEATH the band —
    // `raise(below)` with the band's floor — so the band always wins.
    // `lowestMini` is that floor: the lowest-z SETTLED minimized window (a
    // homing/restoring window is leaving the band, so it's excluded → the
    // restoring window comes up to the front of the non-minimized band).

    lowestMini() {
        const ws = this.wins.children
        for (let i = 0; i < ws.length; i++) { const w = ws[i]; if (this.isMin(w) && !this.homingOf(w)) return w }
        return null
        },

    // bring a freshly-opened window to the front of the NON-minimized band

    frontOpen(w) { frontWin = w; w.raise(this.lowestMini()) },

    // seatedness for a tile's badge: the window's visual width equals the
    // slot's — geometry, not bookkeeping. Deps arrive as arguments (the
    // windowItems pattern); winSeq re-derives it at every journey boundary
    // (miniGo's completion nudges it), pointer/hot cover the wave frames.

    seatCheck(wins, i, slotW, seq, px, hot) {
        const w = wins != null && i >= 0 && i < wins.length ? wins[i] : null
        if (w == null || w.homing == true) return false   // homing = leaving now: the badge goes first
        return w.scale < 0.9 && Math.abs(w.scale * w.width - slotW) < 0.5
        },

    nudge() { winSeq = winSeq + 1 },

    restoreMini(w) {
        if (w == null || !this.isMin(w) || this.homingOf(w)) return   // already on the way home
        this.setHoming(w, true)
        this.startUnpark(w)                      // the slot stays mine until the journey home ends
        this.bringFront(w)                       // frontmost from the first frame of the journey
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        },

    // miniBack's onStop: the window is home — release the slot and focus it

    unparked(w) {
        this.setIx(w, -1)
        this.setHoming(w, false)
        this.rebuildMinis()
        this.focusWin(w)
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        },

    restoreMiniAt(i) {
        const ws = this.miniList()
        if (i >= 0 && i < ws.length) this.restoreMini(ws[i])
        },

    openAbout(a) {
        winCount = winCount + 1
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        const w = this.createView("AboutWindow", this.wins, ({ forApp: "" + a,
            wx: (app.width - 280) / 2 + (winCount % 3) * 16, wy: 110 + (winCount % 3) * 14 }))
        this.frontOpen(w)
        },

    // a press that lands on nothing deeper (the wallpaper) also dismisses —
    // the App root is the sink of last resort

    dismissAbouts() {
        const doomed = this.wins.children.filter((w) => this.isPlain(w))
        for (const w of doomed) this.closeWin(w)
        },

    onMouseDown() { this.dismissAbouts() },

    darkMode: boolean = false,

    newFinder() {
        this.dismissAbouts()
        winCount = winCount + 1
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        // spawn positions cascade macOS-style, wrapping after a handful
        const w = this.createView("FinderWindow", this.wins, ({ wx: 90 + (winCount % 7) * 28, wy: 46 + (winCount % 7) * 26 }))
        this.frontOpen(w)
        },

    // Every open verb takes `v`, the view the open came from (the
    // double-clicked row or icon): the window zooms from its center — the
    // macOS behavior — and shrinks back to it on close. With no
    // origin (menu, dock, ⌘O) a window zooms gently from its own center.
    // originOf resolves that center in app coords by walking the parents.

    originOf(v) {
        if (v == null) return null
        let ox = v.width / 2
        let oy = v.height / 2
        let n = v
        while (n != null) { ox += n.x || 0; oy += n.y || 0; n = n.parent }
        return ({ x: ox, y: oy })
        },

    // A first open (count 0 — or 0 again after Quit) is a launch: the dock
    // icon bounces (Apple's timing) and the window appears mid-second-bounce,
    // as a fast-launching Mac app's does. Later opens are instant.

    openViewer(p, nm, v) {
        if (p == "") return
        const first = !mdRunning && !mdLaunching
        if (first) mdLaunching = true
        const go = () => {
            mdLaunching = false
            mdRunning = true
            viewerCount = viewerCount + 1
            this.dismissAbouts()
            winCount = winCount + 1
            winSeq = winSeq + 1
            const wx = 160 + (winCount % 7) * 28
            const wy = 36 + (winCount % 7) * 26
            const o = this.originOf(v)
            const w = this.createView("ViewerWindow", this.wins, ({ path: p, title: "" + nm, wx: wx, wy: wy,
                bornX: o != null ? o.x - wx : -1, bornY: o != null ? o.y - wy - this.menuBarH : -1,
                bornFrom: v != null ? v : null }))
            this.frontOpen(w)
            }
        if (first) { this.dock.row.viewerIcon.poke(); setTimeout(go, 900) }
        else go()
        },

    // a full Declare app in a window — the path is demos/-relative (the slot
    // convention): "../../calendar/calendar" runs the real calendar,
    // "../../viewer/viewer" the Declare Viewer. `envExtra` appends to the
    // window's env segment ("program=…"). The two hosted apps count
    // separately (running dots + Quit are per application), and each has a
    // dock icon that launch-bounces on its first open.

    calCount: number = 0,
    dvCount: number = 0,

    // running is per application and survives its windows: set when a launch
    // completes, cleared only by Quit — the dock dots read exactly these

    mdRunning: boolean = false,
    calRunning: boolean = false,
    dvRunning: boolean = false,
    stRunning: boolean = false,

    launching: boolean = false,                  // calendar mid-launch-bounce
    dvLaunching: boolean = false,                // Declare Viewer mid-launch-bounce
    mdLaunching: boolean = false,                // markdown mid-launch-bounce
    stLaunching: boolean = false,                // Stickies mid-launch-bounce

    openApp(p, nm, v, envExtra) {
        const isCal = ("" + p).indexOf("calendar") >= 0
        // the launch gate: flags move inside go(), so the running dot appears
        // when the app has launched (mid-second-bounce), not at the click —
        // the mid-launch flag guards a double-click during the bounce
        const first = !(isCal ? calRunning : dvRunning) && !(isCal ? launching : dvLaunching)
        if (first) { if (isCal) launching = true; else dvLaunching = true }
        const go = () => {
            if (isCal) { launching = false; calRunning = true; calCount = calCount + 1 }
            else { dvLaunching = false; dvRunning = true; dvCount = dvCount + 1 }
            this.dismissAbouts()
            winCount = winCount + 1
            winSeq = winSeq + 1
            const wx = 110 + (winCount % 5) * 30
            const wy = 24 + (winCount % 5) * 22
            // the calendar wants room — a month grid reads best wide; the
            // viewer keeps the base app-window size
            const ww = isCal ? 1000 : 880
            const wh = isCal ? 680 : 560
            const o = this.originOf(v)
            const w = this.createView("AppWindow", this.wins, ({ appPath: "" + p, title: "" + nm, wx: wx, wy: wy,
                width: ww, height: wh,
                extraEnv: envExtra != null ? "" + envExtra : "",
                bornX: o != null ? o.x - wx : -1, bornY: o != null ? o.y - wy - this.menuBarH : -1,
                bornFrom: v != null ? v : null }))
            this.frontOpen(w)
            }
        if (first) { (isCal ? this.dock.row.calIcon : this.dock.row.dvIcon).poke(); setTimeout(go, 900) }
        else go()
        },

    // ── Stickies: the built-in application — its notes are StickyWindows in
    // this very program, no island involved. The welcome note pre-opens at
    // boot (so Stickies boots running, dot lit, no bounce — it was already
    // up when you arrived); later launches bounce like any app's.

    welcomeNote: string = """
**Hello!** This is a working desktop — windows, menus, a dock — running entirely in your browser.

It's built with **Declare**, a new language for user interfaces. A Declare program doesn't script behavior; it states what the interface *is* — this note's yellow, that window's shadow — and the runtime keeps every relationship true while you interact.

**Try things.** Drag the windows. Open apps from the dock. Minimize something. Browse the language's documentation in the Documentation window.

**Curious how it's made?** Press the `[ ]` icon in the dock to read this desktop's own source.
""",

    newSticky(txt, v) {
        const first = !stRunning && !stLaunching
        if (first) stLaunching = true
        const go = () => {
            stLaunching = false
            stRunning = true
            this.dismissAbouts()
            winCount = winCount + 1
            winSeq = winSeq + 1
            const w = this.createView("StickyWindow", this.wins, ({ note: "" + (txt || "*A new note.*"),
                wx: 480 + (winCount % 5) * 26, wy: 90 + (winCount % 5) * 24 }))
            this.frontOpen(w)
            }
        if (first) { this.dock.row.stIcon.poke(); setTimeout(go, 900) }
        else go()
        },

    openWelcome() {
        stRunning = true
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        const w = this.createView("StickyWindow", this.wins, ({ note: this.welcomeNote,
            width: 400, height: 345, wx: Math.max(760, app.width - 424), wy: 120 }))
        this.frontOpen(w)
        },

    closeWin(w) { w.beginClose() },              // the reverse zoom; removal on its onStop

    reallyClose(w) {
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        if (w.constructor.name == "AppWindow") {
            if (this.appOf(w) == "calendar") calCount = calCount - 1
            else dvCount = dvCount - 1
            }
        if (frontWin == w) frontWin = null
        if (w.constructor.name == "ViewerWindow") viewerCount = viewerCount - 1
        this.wins.removeChild(w)
        w.discard()
        this.rebuildMinis()                      // a Quit can take a minimized window's tile with it
        },

    // the menu-bar clock is live — a hardcoded time sitting one pixel column
    // from the real menu bar's is the first thing the eye catches

    clockText: string = "",

    dayName: string = "",
    dayNum: number = 0,

    tick() {
        const d = new Date()
        const wd = ["Sun", "Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat"][d.getDay()]
        const mo = ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"][d.getMonth()]
        const h12 = d.getHours() % 12 == 0 ? 12 : d.getHours() % 12
        const mm = (d.getMinutes() < 10 ? "0" : "") + d.getMinutes()
        clockText = wd + " " + mo + " " + d.getDate() + " " + h12 + ":" + mm + " " + (d.getHours() >= 12 ? "PM" : "AM")
        dayName = wd.toUpperCase()
        dayNum = d.getDate()
        },

    // ── the documentation model: one fetch of the docs app's own spine; the
    // Finder's Guide + Reference folders and the synthesized reference pages
    // all derive from it ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

    docsModel: DataSource [ url = "../../apps/docs/docs-model.json" ],

    buildRef(name) {
        const m = this.docsModel.value
        if (m == null || m.nodes == null) return "# " + name + "\n\nLoading the documentation model…"
        const n = m.nodes[name]
        let md = "# " + name + "\n\n" + (n != null && n.doc ? "" + n.doc : "*(no element doc)*")
        const pre = name + "."
        const keys = Object.keys(m.nodes).filter((k) => k.indexOf(pre) == 0)
        if (keys.length > 0) {
            md = md + "\n\n---\n\n## Attributes\n"
            for (const k of keys) {
                const d = ("" + (m.nodes[k].doc || "")).split("\n\n")[0]
                md = md + "\n**" + k.slice(pre.length) + "** — " + d + "\n"
                }
            }
        return md
        },

    // 15s ticks catch the minute flip well within a minute-precision display

    // the welcome note is deferred one tick: its position derives from
    // app.width, which is not yet live inside onInit (the host wires sizes
    // after construction) — an immediate open would land on the fallback floor

    onInit() { this.docsModel.fetch(); this.newFinder(); setTimeout(() => this.openWelcome(), 0); this.tick(); setInterval(() => this.tick(), 15000) },

    // ── ⌘-shortcuts dispatch from the menus' own records (the Newton
    // direction, planes.md §9): one registry, derived from whatever the
    // contextual bar currently shows, so the active application's keys are
    // automatically the live set — nothing declared twice. (The hold-⌘ HUD
    // that proved the model is retired from this Mac-faithful desktop; these
    // records are exactly the registry it needs to come back.)

    keyRegistry: array = { (app.menus || [])
        .flatMap((m) => (m.items || []).filter((r) => r.key).map((r) => ({ menu: "" + m.label, ...r }))) },

    onKeyDown(e) <- Keys {
        if (!e.meta || e.repeat || e.key == "Meta") return
        if (this.bar.mb.shown) return                // an open menu owns the keyboard
        const combo = "⌘" + (e.key.length == 1 ? e.key.toUpperCase() : e.key)
        const hit = (this.keyRegistry || []).find((r) => r.key == combo && r.enabled != false)
        if (hit != null) this.command("" + hit.id)
        },

    // ── the wallpaper: an original flowing composition, drawn ──────────────
    //
    // A blue linear base with warm and cool radial glows floated over it —
    // real Canvas gradients (a transparent outer stop is the soft falloff),
    // overlapping into a varied, aurora-like field. Handler-less, so a desktop
    // press still dismisses an About box.

    wallpaper: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height },
        draw(d) {
            const W = this.width
            const H = this.height
            const dark = app.darkMode
            // the blue base — a vertical linear gradient, deep at top
            const base = d.createLinearGradient(0, 0, 0, H)
            base.addColorStop(0, dark ? "#161c2e" : "#3a5c9c")
            base.addColorStop(1, dark ? "#0c1220" : "#6298ce")
            d.fillStyle = base
            d.fillRect(0, 0, W, H)
            // each glow: a radial gradient from a translucent core to a fully
            // transparent rim — the falloff is the gradient, no stacking
            const span = Math.max(W, H)
            const a = dark ? 0.11 : 0.22
            const glow = (cx, cy, radius, rgb, core) => {
                const g = d.createRadialGradient(cx, cy, 0, cx, cy, radius)
                g.addColorStop(0, "rgba(" + rgb + "," + core + ")")
                g.addColorStop(1, "rgba(" + rgb + ",0)")
                d.fillStyle = g
                d.fillRect(0, 0, W, H)
                }
            glow(W * 0.16, H * 0.20, span * 0.55, "255,138,96", a)    // warm coral, upper-left
            glow(W * 0.86, H * 0.28, span * 0.50, "92,208,228", a)    // cyan, upper-right
            glow(W * 0.64, H * 0.90, span * 0.60, "150,108,222", a)   // violet, lower
            glow(W * 0.42, H * 0.55, span * 0.35, "255,202,128", a * 0.55)  // warm highlight, center
            },
        ],

    // ── the windows layer: a structural container; order is promotion ──────

    wins: View [ x = 0, y = { app.menuBarH }, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height - app.menuBarH } ],

    winSeq: number = 0,                          // bumped on every open/close, so window lists re-derive

    // ── the menu bar: whole menus as records, and contextual — the array
    // re-derives from the active application (and from winSeq/frontWin for
    // the Window menu), so the bar follows focus the way the Mac's does,
    // live even while a menu is open ───────────────────────────────────────

    menus: array = { [
        ({ id: "brand", label: "Declare", kind: "BrandMark", items: [
            ({ id: "about-desktop", label: "About Declare Desktop", icon: "▢" }),
            ({ divider: true }),
            ({ id: "dark", label: "Dark Mode", key: "⌘K", icon: "◐", checked: app.darkMode }),
            ({ divider: true }),
            ({ id: "source-desktop", label: "View & Edit Source", icon: "‹›" }),
        ] }),
        ({ id: "app", label: this.nameOf(this.activeApp), bold: true,
            items: this.appMenuItems(this.activeApp) }),
        ({ id: "file", label: "File", items: [
            ({ id: "new", label: "New", key: "⌘N" }),
            ({ id: "open", label: "Open…", key: "⌘O", enabled: this.activeApp != "calendar" }),
            ({ divider: true }),
            ({ id: "close", label: "Close Window", key: "⌘W", enabled: app.frontWin != null }),
        ] }),

        // the standard Edit suite — present and keyed, honestly disabled:
        // nothing on this desktop edits yet

        ({ id: "edit", label: "Edit", items: [
            ({ id: "undo", label: "Undo", key: "⌘Z", enabled: false }),
            ({ id: "redo", label: "Redo", key: "⇧⌘Z", enabled: false }),
            ({ divider: true }),
            ({ id: "cut", label: "Cut", key: "⌘X", enabled: false }),
            ({ id: "copy", label: "Copy", key: "⌘C", enabled: false }),
            ({ id: "paste", label: "Paste", key: "⌘V", enabled: false }),
            ({ divider: true }),
            ({ id: "selall", label: "Select All", key: "⌘A", enabled: false }),
        ] }),
        ({ id: "window", label: "Window", items: this.windowItems(this.winSeq, app.frontWin) }),
        ({ id: "help", label: "Help", items: [
            ({ id: "hsearch", kind: "HelpSearch" }),
            ({ id: "help-app", label: this.nameOf(this.activeApp) + " Help", icon: "lightbulb" }),
        ] }),
    ] },

    // the application menu: About, source, and — except for Files, which
    // like the real Finder is always running — Quit

    appMenuItems(a) {
        const nm = this.nameOf(a)
        const out = []
        out.push(({ id: "about-app", label: "About " + nm }))
        out.push(({ divider: true }))
        out.push(({ id: "source", label: "View & Edit Source" }))
        if (a != "files") {
            out.push(({ divider: true }))
            out.push(({ id: "quit", label: "Quit " + nm, key: "⌘Q" }))
            }
        return out
        },

    // the Window menu is live state as records: the active application's open
    // windows, front one checked. `seq` and `front` arrive as arguments so
    // the menus constraint above visibly reads them — its re-derive hooks.

    isPlain(w) { return w != null && w.plain == true },      // any-typed seams for a View-typed list
    titleOf(w) { return w != null && w.title ? "" + w.title : "" },

    windowItems(seq, front) {
        const a = this.activeApp
        const ws = this.wins.children
        const out = []
        for (let i = 0; i < ws.length; i++) {
            if (this.isPlain(ws[i]) || this.appOf(ws[i]) != a) continue
            out.push(({ id: "win:" + i, label: this.titleOf(ws[i]) || this.nameOf(a), checked: ws[i] == front }))
            }
        if (out.length == 0) out.push(({ id: "nowin", label: "No Windows", enabled: false }))
        return out
        },

    bar: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { app.width }, height = { app.menuBarH }, fill = { theme.surface }, opacity = 0.92,
        mb: MenuBar [ x = 10, height = { parent.height }, menus = { app.menus },
            picked(id) { app.command(id) },
            ],
        clock: Text [ x = { parent.width - this.width - 14 }, y = center, fontSize = 13,
            fontWeight = medium,                 // 400 reads thin under browser antialiasing; medium matches the platform density
            textColor = { theme.text }, text = { app.clockText } ],
        ],

    // ── the dock ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    dock: View [ height = 150,
        width = { this.plate.width },
        x = { (app.width - this.width) / 2 },
        y = { app.height - 155.5 },           // plate (88 tall, at the dock's bottom) sits 5.5px off the screen edge — measured

        // the enter/exit envelope the icons scale by — sprung fast (~100ms), so
        // entry reads as one quick motion; inside the strip the icons track
        // the pointer directly.
        // The band has hysteresis: it arms at 96px above the bottom edge, but
        // while hot it holds to 118 — past a magnified icon's crest (~106).
        // Without it, hovering the top of a grown icon sat above the arming
        // line, collapsing the dock under the pointer — a pump the real dock
        // (whose hover region follows the magnified extent) never shows.

        // `hovering` gates the whole band: when the pointer leaves the
        // browser window the platform clears it, so the dock cools instead
        // of staying magnified at the pointer's last known column

        armBand: number = 100,                   // the hover band arms this far above the bottom edge (~plate top at 93.5)…
        holdBand: number = 132,                  // …and holds to here while hot (past a magnified icon's crest ~104)
        hot: number = 0,

        // hotGate is the INSTANT band result (0/1). The app icons read it as
        // their wave gate, so their own per-icon mag springs do all the enter/
        // leave smoothing (no double-spring). `hot` is the SPRUNG version — the
        // mini section (a shared pointer-spring layout) still eases through it.

        hotGate: number = { app.hovering && app.pointerY > app.height - (this.hot > 0.5 ? this.holdBand : this.armBand) ? 1 : 0 },

        heat: Spring [ attribute = hot, to = { parent.hotGate }, stiffness = 3600, damping = 120, epsilon = 0.002 ],

        // at rest the icons sit clearly inside the plate; a magnified icon
        // crests above its top — bottom-anchored, top rises. The row must be
        // dock-height: the icons' bottom anchor reads parent.height, and a
        // shorter row would float them above the plate at rest.

        plate: View [ x = 0, y = { parent.height - 88 }, height = 88, width = { parent.row.width + 32 + app.miniSpan + app.miniMagX },
            cornerRadius = 27, fill = { app.darkMode ? 0x2A2A30 : 0xFCFCFE }, opacity = 0.4 ],

        // the plate's edge rim — DRAWN, at full strength (the plate is
        // translucent, so the rim rides a sibling above it, not inside its
        // opacity). A top-lit inner highlight: a vertical white gradient stroke
        // brightest along the TOP edge and fading down the sides to nothing at
        // the floor — the "lit from above" bevel the real dock carries
        // (measured: edge lum ~185 over the ~138 fill, fading through the
        // corners as the rim winds around). A faint outer hairline sets the
        // plate off from the wallpaper.

        plateRim: View [ x = 0, y = { parent.height - 88 }, height = 88, width = { parent.plate.width },
            draw(d) {
                const w = this.width
                const h = this.height
                const r = 27
                d.strokeStyle = app.darkMode ? "rgba(0,0,0,0.34)" : "rgba(112,124,148,0.22)"
                d.lineWidth = 1
                d.beginPath()
                d.roundRect(0.5, 0.5, w - 1, h - 1, r - 0.5)
                d.stroke()
                const g = d.createLinearGradient(0, 0, 0, h)
                g.addColorStop(0, app.darkMode ? "rgba(255,255,255,0.30)" : "rgba(255,255,255,0.62)")
                g.addColorStop(0.42, app.darkMode ? "rgba(255,255,255,0.05)" : "rgba(255,255,255,0.13)")
                g.addColorStop(1, "rgba(255,255,255,0)")
                d.strokeStyle = g
                d.lineWidth = 1.5
                d.beginPath()
                d.roundRect(1.4, 1.4, w - 2.8, h - 2.8, r - 1.4)
                d.stroke()
                },
            ],
        row: View [ x = 16, y = 0, height = { parent.height },
            layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 16 ],

            // a running app's icon raises its open windows instead of opening
            // more — dockOpen: raise if any, else the app's default open
            // (which itself decides launch-bounce vs instant by the flag)

            DockIcon [ ix = 0, name = "Finder", kind = "files", hue1 = #59B0F6, hue2 = #1D6FD1,
                running = true,
                onClick() { app.dockOpen("files", () => app.newFinder()) },
                ],
            viewerIcon: DockIcon [ ix = 1, name = "Markdown", glyph = "M↓", hue1 = #8E7CE8, hue2 = #5A46B8,
                running = { app.mdRunning },
                onClick() { app.dockOpen("markdown", () => app.openViewer("../../docs/declare.md", "declare.md")) },
                ],

            // Guide is a document icon (no running state): it always opens
            // its document — in the Markdown application

            DockIcon [ ix = 2, name = "Guide", kind = "pencil", hue1 = #F49B45, hue2 = #C96B1D,
                onClick() { app.openViewer("../../docs/guide/01-thinking-in-declare.md", "Thinking in Declare") },
                ],

            // the Declare Viewer as a dock application: launches (bounce, dot,
            // Quit) like the others, hosting the real apps/viewer as an
            // embedded app — opened, by default, on this program's own source

            dvIcon: DockIcon [ ix = 3, name = "Declare Viewer", glyph = "[  ]", gsize = 0.42,
                hue1 = #2E6FE0, hue2 = #12A594,   // the brand's blue→teal accent thread
                running = { app.dvRunning },
                onClick() { app.dockOpen("declareviewer", () => app.openApp("../../viewer/viewer", "Declare Viewer", this, "program=desktop.declare")) },
                ],

            // the plausible-Mac-application set: enough items for the
            // magnification wave to read like the real dock's

            calIcon: DockIcon [ ix = 4, name = "Calendar", kind = "calendar", hue1 = #FFFFFF, hue2 = #EFEFF2,
                running = { app.calRunning },
                onClick() { app.dockOpen("calendar", () => app.openApp("../../calendar/calendar", "Calendar", this, "base=../calendar/")) },
                ],
            stIcon: DockIcon [ ix = 5, name = "Stickies", kind = "stickies", hue1 = #FCF4A7, hue2 = #F3E27E,
                running = { app.stRunning },
                onClick() { app.dockOpen("stickies", () => app.newSticky("", this)) },
                ],
            DockIcon [ ix = 6, name = "Mail", kind = "mail", hue1 = #1E8CFB, hue2 = #1667D9 ],
            DockIcon [ ix = 7, name = "Music", glyph = "♪", gsize = 0.52, hue1 = #FB5C74, hue2 = #FA233B ],
            DockIcon [ ix = 8, name = "Terminal", kind = "terminal", hue1 = #3A3A40, hue2 = #17171B ],
            ],

        // ── the minimized-window section (the Mac's): a vertical divider,
        // then a hit tile per minimized window — the visuals are the parked
        // windows themselves, riding the same wave (Window.miniCx). The
        // section exists only while the sprung span is open; the divider
        // fades with the widening, so growth reads as one motion, no seam.

        minis: View [ visible = { app.miniSpan > 0.5 },
            x = { parent.row.x + parent.row.width + 16 }, y = 0, height = { parent.height },
            div: View [ x = 0, y = { parent.height - 77 }, width = 1, height = 65,
                opacity = { Math.min(1, app.miniSpan / 26) },
                inkD: Color = #FFFFFF48,
                inkL: Color = #00000042,
                fill = { app.darkMode ? this.inkD : this.inkL },
                ],

            // a static pool of tiles, not replication: each is a pure
            // constraint reader over the app's mini state, positioned from
            // the shared layout — there is no per-row machinery to go stale

            rowM: View [ x = 0, y = 0, height = { parent.height },
                MiniTile [ ix = 0 ],
                MiniTile [ ix = 1 ],
                MiniTile [ ix = 2 ],
                MiniTile [ ix = 3 ],
                MiniTile [ ix = 4 ],
                MiniTile [ ix = 5 ],
                ],
            ],
        ],
    ]
